title: Intelligence Briefing Dashboard
subtitle: Weekly Conflict Monitor · All Regions
sources: Reuters · AP News · Al Jazeera · ACLED · US Embassy Alerts · LiveUAMap · BNO News · ISW · British Defence Intelligence · Kyiv Post · Sudan Tribune · Irrawaddy · CNN · NBC · CBS · Fox News · HRW · UN News · CFR

regions.name: Middle East
overview: Lebanon framework signed June 26: US Secretary Rubio announced Israel and Lebanon signed a framework agreement for 'lasting peace and security'; Israel to withdraw from two areas in southern Lebanon; framework requires Hezbollah disarmament and IDF withdrawal; Hezbollah was NOT party to talks — the same structural incompatibility that has doomed three previous ceasefires; trilateral Military Coordination Group established.

Iran nuclear standoff: IAEA-Iran inspection deadlock persists — Grossi confirmed June 24 that inspections 'will happen' but Iran insists access only after a final deal; US-Iran technical talks expected to resume in Switzerland June 29-30; 60-day Burgenstock road map clock running with key verification question unresolved.

Gaza intractable: IDF ceasefire violations exceed 3,338 since October 2025 truce; 992+ Palestinians killed, 3,138 injured; only 36% of allocated aid trucks entered; Lebanon framework negotiations absorb diplomatic bandwidth leaving Gaza without a political resolution pathway; humanitarian conditions at critical thresholds.

Houthi Red Sea standdown continues: Nine consecutive months without maritime attacks since September 2025; MARAD 2026-006 remains in force; Houthi hardliner faction monitoring Iran nuclear talks outcome — any perceived Iranian capitulation on enrichment strengthens MOU-sceptic faction internally.

countries.name: Gaza
incidents: 27 Jun — Gaza: IDF ceasefire violations exceed 3,338 since October 2025 truce per Government Media Office; attacks by air, artillery, and direct shooting have killed at least 992 Palestinians and injured 3,138 since ceasefire.

26 Jun — Gaza City: IDF strikes four newly built Hamas rocket-firing positions constructed post-ceasefire; UN confirms only 36% of allocated aid trucks (52,129 of 144,000) have entered since October 2025 — 64% below allocated levels.

24 Jun — Khan Younis: IDF targeted killing campaign continues; between May 29-June 10 IDF eliminated 15 Hamas operatives including Gaza City Brigade deputy commander and Hamas squad commander operating as hospital doctor.

22 Jun — Rafah: Egypt-facilitated convoy admits 140 trucks through restricted Rafah crossing in 48-hour window; UN warns of imminent famine conditions as aid throughput remains critically below allocated levels.
countries.coa: The October 2025 ceasefire remains nominal — violations exceed 3,338 and IDF continues operations against Hamas military infrastructure. Lebanon framework negotiations absorb US and regional diplomatic bandwidth, leaving Gaza without a resolution pathway. No political endpoint within 90 days; humanitarian conditions at critical thresholds requiring emergency escalation.

countries.name: Lebanon
incidents: 26 Jun — Washington: US Secretary Rubio announces Israel and Lebanon signed framework agreement for 'lasting peace and security'; Israel to withdraw from two southern Lebanon areas; agreement creates trilateral Military Coordination Group; Hezbollah was NOT party to the talks.

26 Jun — Jerusalem: Israeli envoy clarifies IDF withdrawal is not based on fixed timetable but contingent on Hezbollah disarmament and evacuation of all Hezbollah operatives; Netanyahu announces pilot zones where Lebanese Armed Forces take exclusive control.

24 Jun — Beirut: Lebanese government signs framework agreement under US mediation; pilot zones designated; Lebanon parliament pressing for binding IDF withdrawal timeline as precondition for Hezbollah compliance.

22 Jun — Southern Lebanon: IDF continues to hold southern Lebanon buffer zone; Hezbollah has not been part of Washington talks; structural incompatibility between framework terms and Hezbollah's red lines persists.
countries.coa: The June 26 framework agreement is the most structured peace framework yet — Israel committed to two-area withdrawal, Lebanon signed, trilateral Military Coordination Group established. However, Hezbollah was not party to talks and has previously rejected disarmament without simultaneous IDF withdrawal. Pilot zone transfers are the first verifiable indicator of implementation. HIGH maintained; if Hezbollah formally accepts framework terms, downgrade to MODERATE possible within 60 days.

countries.name: Iran
incidents: 27 Jun — Washington: US Secretary Rubio states technical talks expected to resume in Switzerland June 29-30; 60-day Burgenstock road map under active implementation; uranium stockpile and centrifuge status remain unverified.

24 Jun — Vienna: IAEA Director-General Grossi says inspections of Fordow and Natanz 'are going to happen'; states MoU 'explicitly' calls for IAEA supervision; dates and locations 'being discussed in collaboration with the government of Iran.'

24 Jun — Tehran: Senior Iranian official Gharibabadi states inspection issues 'will be reviewed and decided only within a final agreement'; insists access can occur only after sanctions ended — making verification conditional on Iranian goodwill.
22 Jun — Burgenstock: 60-day road map operative; Qatar and Pakistan facilitating; IRGC Syria drawdown phase-1 confirmed complete; technical teams preparing for June 29-30 Switzerland talks.
countries.coa: The IAEA-Iran inspection standoff — confirmed active June 24-27 — is the central obstacle to finalising the Burgenstock road map within the 60-day window. Iran conditions inspection access on a final deal, meaning the IAEA cannot verify uranium stockpile or centrifuge status until after a deal. June 29-30 Switzerland technical talks are the next critical indicator. CRITICAL maintained.

countries.name: Yemen
incidents: 27 Jun — Red Sea: MARAD 2026-006 remains in force; nine consecutive months without maritime attacks since September 2025; insurance premiums elevated; Cape of Good Hope diversions continuing.

25 Jun — Sana'a: Houthi hardliner faction actively monitoring IAEA-Iran standoff; MOU-sceptics argue perceived Iranian nuclear concessions justify resuming Red Sea operations; internal debate ongoing.
23 Jun — Bab al-Mandab: Houthi patrol vessels maintaining surveillance posture; no confirmed attack preparations; formal readiness posture held pending Iran-US talks outcome.
20 Jun — New York: UN Security Council receives Secretary-General report on Houthi maritime activities; UNSC notes nine-month operational pause but maintains resolution framework.
countries.coa: Nine months of no Red Sea attacks is the longest operational pause of the conflict. The IAEA-Iran inspection standoff is the primary reactivation trigger — strengthening Houthi hardliners who argue Iranian concessions on enrichment justify resumed operations. June 29-30 Switzerland technical talks are a critical indicator. HIGH maintained; reversal risk is significant if Iran makes major nuclear concessions.

countries.name: Israel
incidents: 26 Jun — Washington: Netanyahu signs Lebanon framework agreement; announces IDF withdrawal from two southern Lebanon areas; far-right coalition partners Smotrich and Ben-Gvir demand no withdrawal without full Hezbollah disarmament first.

26 Jun — Jerusalem: Israeli envoy clarifies withdrawal not on fixed timetable; contingent on Hezbollah full disarmament and evacuation; structural tension between US pressure and coalition imperatives persists.
24 Jun — Gaza: IDF targeted killing campaign continues; coalition imperative maintains operational pace; ceasefire violations now exceed 3,338.

22 Jun — Southern Lebanon: IDF buffer zone positions reinforced; additional intelligence assets deployed; Netanyahu facing simultaneous Lebanon framework implementation demands and Gaza operational pressures.
countries.coa: Israel's coalition dynamics remain the primary MOU instability driver. Netanyahu's far-right partners continue demanding operations; the Lebanon framework agreement creates a new compliance test. Hezbollah non-consent to the framework provides an exit ramp for resumed strikes. HIGH maintained.

countries.name: Iraq
incidents: 27 Jun — Baghdad: PMF reviewing Lebanon framework agreement implications; formal compliance guidance from Tehran expected after June 29-30 Switzerland nuclear talks; Kata'ib Hezbollah operational tempo unchanged.
24 Jun — Baghdad: PMF awaiting Tehran guidance on revised posture in light of Lebanon framework; Iran-affiliated factions monitoring nuclear talks closely.
22 Jun — Salah ad-Din: ISIS attack on civilian checkpoint; 2 security forces killed; IED emplacement activity elevated in Anbar and Diyala provinces.
20 Jun — Anbar Province: US-Iraq joint counter-terrorism operation disrupts ISIS logistics route near Al-Qa'im; weapons cache and communications equipment recovered.
countries.coa: PMF posture will shift materially only after Iran-US nuclear talks produce concrete Iranian concessions. Lebanon framework and June 29-30 Switzerland talks are the near-term indicators. ISIS Anbar-Salah ad-Din reconstitution continues independently and remains a growing secondary concern.

countries.name: Saudi Arabia
incidents: 27 Jun — Riyadh: Saudi Arabia monitoring Lebanon framework agreement with cautious optimism; Aramco logistics fully normalized via Hormuz; Saudi-Iran rapprochement track advancing.
26 Jun — Riyadh: Saudi Arabia tracks Iran-US technical talks expected June 29-30; independent interest in verified IAEA access to Iranian enrichment sites as precondition for regional security.
24 Jun — Gulf: Hormuz commercial shipping fully restored; Lloyd's war-risk premiums stabilising; Saudi energy ministry maintaining normal routing.
22 Jun — Riyadh: Saudi-Iran bilateral rapprochement continuing under Burgenstock framework; economic normalization agenda advancing; US 5th Fleet freedom-of-navigation operations maintained.
countries.coa: Saudi Arabia's best-case trajectory — regional normalization under a durable US-Iran agreement — remains on track. Lebanon framework agreement reduces near-term Hezbollah pressure on Aramco infrastructure. If nuclear inspection dispute derails talks, Houthi resumption risk returns and Hormuz exposure re-emerges. HIGH maintained pending nuclear talks outcome.

countries.name: Syria
incidents: 27 Jun — Eastern Euphrates: ISIS attack on SDF checkpoint; 2 SDF fighters killed; cross-border infiltration from Iraq confirmed by US military monitoring.
25 Jun — Deir ez-Zor: ISIS attack on local council compound; 2 guards killed; northeast Syria ISIS reconstitution at highest pace since 2019.
23 Jun — Damascus: IRGC phase-2 drawdown timeline under active negotiation in comprehensive talks framework; limited IRGC advisory presence in western Syria remaining.
20 Jun — Homs Province: IRGC phase-1 drawdown confirmed complete; Israeli strike frequency on Syria has decreased commensurate with IRGC withdrawal.
countries.coa: IRGC phase-1 drawdown from Syria is complete, progressively reducing Israeli strike justifications. Phase-2 timeline under comprehensive talks negotiation. ISIS reconstitution in eastern Syria is a growing independent risk — now at highest pace since 2019. Political resolution remains 90+ days away.

countries.name: Egypt
incidents: 27 Jun — Cairo: Egypt FM monitoring Lebanon framework agreement; Cairo's mediation role in Gaza maintained; Egypt-Hamas mediation track continuing.
25 Jun — New York: Egypt presses UN Security Council for Gaza political resolution; calls Lebanon framework 'progress but incomplete without Palestinian pathway'.
22 Jun — Rafah: Egypt facilitates humanitarian convoy; 140 trucks in 48-hour rolling window under restricted access.
19 Jun — N. Sinai: Security forces arrest 4 suspects linked to Libya-connected jihadist network; weapons cache seized near Rafah border zone.
countries.coa: Egypt's diplomatic role remains strong — Cairo's standing as regional stabiliser is at its highest in years. Rafah aid operations continue under restricted access. No major security escalation expected within 90 days.

countries.name: Jordan
incidents: 27 Jun — Amman: Jordan welcomes Lebanon framework agreement; King Abdullah calls for Gaza political resolution; Jordan-US security coordination maintained.
25 Jun — Amman: Jordan-US security coordination meeting on regional stabilisation implications of Lebanon framework and Iran nuclear talks.
22 Jun — Eastern Border: Security forces intercept cross-border arms cache from Syria near Mafraq; 2 arrested.
20 Jun — Zarqa: GID counterterrorism operation arrests 3 suspects linked to ISIL-connected network planning domestic attack.
countries.coa: Jordan's threat environment remains MODERATE. Cross-border smuggling from Syria and domestic political pressure from unresolved Gaza crisis are primary concerns. Lebanon framework adds modest positive signal. No major escalation expected within 90 days.

countries.name: Libya
incidents: 27 Jun — Tripoli: GNU-LNA UN-mediated talks on oil revenue sharing continue without breakthrough; dual-government paralysis persisting.
25 Jun — Sirte: LNA-GNU tensions near Sirte corridor; ceasefire technically holding but factional confrontations continuing.
23 Jun — Mediterranean: IOM reports 310+ migrants rescued in five operations this week; 8 deaths; Libyan coastal departure rate unchanged.
21 Jun — Benghazi: LNA confirms weapons cache with Sahel armed group connections seized; arms trafficking route to Mali continues unabated.
countries.coa: Libya's dual-government stalemate continues without political breakthrough. GNU-LNA tensions remain periodically kinetic. Arms and migrant flows to Europe and the Sahel continue unabated. No unification pathway within 90 days.

countries.name: Tunisia
incidents: 27 Jun — Tunis: EU-Tunisia relations deteriorating over democratic governance concerns; additional NGO operating licenses revoked.
25 Jun — Tunis: EU formally protests President Saied's latest civil society restrictions; European diplomatic pressure ongoing.
23 Jun — Kasserine: Security forces disrupt suspected JNIM-linked transit cell near Algeria border; 2 arrested.
21 Jun — Sfax: Three migrant departure facilitators detained; Libyan coordination network partially disrupted.
countries.coa: Tunisia faces low-level jihadist threat from Libya-Algeria-linked networks and entrenched political repression under Saied. No major security escalation expected within 90 days.

regions.name: Ukraine/Russia
overview: Ukraine deep-strike campaign constraining Russian logistics: Ukraine's long-range drone and missile strikes on Russian fuel depots, ammunition facilities, and transport networks causing growing supply chain strain in Russian rear; fuel shortages reported in occupied Crimea; Zelensky approved 40-day influence operation aimed at pressuring Russia toward negotiations; Ukraine struck Russian military supply vessels in occupied Crimea.

Pokrovsk remains hottest frontline sector: 257 combat clashes recorded June 26; Russia retains positions in Pokrovsk and Myrnohrad seized in early 2026 but cannot capitalize westward; Ukrainian counterattacks repeatedly complicating Russian expansion — war in attritional manoeuvre phase with drones and logistics interdiction increasingly decisive.

Russia's Black Sea Fleet effectively neutralized: No Russian naval vessels deployed in Black Sea or Sea of Azov; naval aviation now carrying fleet functions; a strategic naval balance inflection point achieved since 2022; Ukraine struck Russian military supply vessels in Crimea as part of influence operation.

Belarus signal repeaters ceased following Zelensky deadline: Kremlin intensifying pressure on Lukashenko for direct military involvement; Lukashenko resisting while maintaining Russia's logistical approval; hybrid pressure on Poland-Belarus border persists at elevated Frontex status.

countries.name: Ukraine
incidents: 27 Jun — Kyiv: President Zelensky approves 40-day influence operation proposed by Security Service of Ukraine aimed at pressuring Russia toward end of war; operation includes strikes on Russian military supply vessels in occupied Crimea.

26 Jun — Frontlines: 257 combat clashes recorded; Pokrovsk and Huliaipole sectors remain hottest; Ukrainian counterattacks complicating Russian efforts to expand control west of Pokrovsk; Kremenchuk district industrial facilities struck by Russian missiles.

25 Jun — Russia (rear): Ukraine's deep-strike campaign on Russian fuel depots, ammunition, and transport networks increasingly effective; fuel shortages in occupied Crimea; disruptions to military supply routes in Russian operational rear.

24 Jun — Odesa Region: Russian forces attack energy and civilian infrastructure of Vylkove Territorial Community; settlements without power; casualties reported; Kyiv Darnytskyi district fire from Russian missile strike extinguished.
countries.coa: Ukraine's deep-strike campaign is constraining Russian frontline sustainability through fuel and ammunition logistics interdiction. The Zelensky-approved 40-day influence operation adds a psychological pressure track. Neither side has operational capacity for a decisive breakthrough. Western arms continuity remains the critical enabler. No major Russian breakthrough expected in 90 days.

countries.name: Russia
incidents: 26 Jun — Ukraine: Russian forces launched over 257 combat engagements; Pokrovsk remains the highest pressure sector; Russia retains positions seized in early 2026 but unable to exploit westward.

25 Jun — Russia (nationwide): Ukraine's deep-strike campaign on Russian logistics causing growing strain on sustainment; ISW assesses Russian intercept consumption costs unsustainable; rear area logistics increasingly constrained.

24 Jun — Crimea: Russia's Black Sea Fleet has no vessels deployed in Black Sea or Sea of Azov; naval aviation carrying fleet functions; Ukraine struck Russian military supply vessels in Crimea as part of influence operation.
23 Jun — Ukraine: Russian forces continue large-scale drone and missile campaign against Ukrainian cities and infrastructure; Poltava and Odesa regions struck in latest wave.
countries.coa: Russia's attritional strategy continues at mounting cost — deep strikes are now systematically constraining rear-area logistics and fuel supply to frontline forces. Russia's 101+ drone capacity demonstrates continued mass-fire capability but intercept costs are unsustainable. The Black Sea Fleet neutralization is a strategic loss. No major Russian operational breakthrough expected in the 90-day window.

countries.name: Belarus
incidents: 27 Jun — Minsk: Kremlin intensifying pressure on Lukashenko to expand Belarusian military involvement in the war; Lukashenko continues resisting direct commitment while maintaining Russia's logistical approval.

25 Jun — Belarus-Ukraine border: Belarusian signal repeaters along Ukrainian border ceased operations following Zelensky deadline; reduces Russian drone navigation support in the border zone — likely temporary under Kremlin pressure.
23 Jun — Poland-Belarus border: Hybrid pressure continues; 280+ crossing attempts in 72-hour period; Frontex elevated status maintained.
20 Jun — Minsk: Belarus maintaining primary Russian logistics enabler role while avoiding direct military involvement; Russian ammunition and fuel transit continuing.
countries.coa: Belarus remains a primary Russian logistics enabler. The signal repeater shutdown reduces Russian drone navigation support but is likely temporary under Kremlin pressure. Lukashenko will not commit Belarusian forces directly to the war. Hybrid pressure on Poland and Baltic borders continues as a low-cost destabilisation tool.

countries.name: Moldova
incidents: 27 Jun — Chisinau: Moldova formally aligned with EU positions on Lebanon framework; Western integration trajectory intact.
25 Jun — Chisinau: EU accession screening chapter process advancing; Moldova on formal accession timeline with strong institutional support.
23 Jun — Transnistria: Unusual logistics movements near Tiraspol continue; Moldova maintains request for increased EU monitoring mission presence.
20 Jun — Chisinau: Energy contingency supply routes from Romania and Greece fully operational; no remaining Russian gas dependency.
countries.coa: Moldova faces moderate risk from Russian hybrid pressure and Transnistria destabilisation. EU accession track provides political insulation. No kinetic escalation expected within 90 days.

countries.name: Georgia
incidents: 27 Jun — Tbilisi: Georgia excluded from US-Armenia strategic cooperation framework signed this week; Georgian Dream Russia alignment accelerating; EU accession suspended since June 18.
25 Jun — Tbilisi: VP Vance visited Armenia and Azerbaijan in shuttle diplomacy without visiting Tbilisi; Georgia increasingly sidelined from US South Caucasus policy.
23 Jun — S. Ossetia: Russian military patrol observed within 200m of administrative boundary line; Georgia files formal diplomatic protest.
21 Jun — Tbilisi: 12 additional pro-EU activists detained in ongoing demonstrations; EU and US issue joint statement demanding release.
countries.coa: Georgia faces dual threats: Russian military proximity in occupied territories and an accelerating internal political crisis as Georgian Dream consolidates Moscow alignment. Georgia increasingly sidelined from US South Caucasus policy. EU and NATO integration pathways at serious risk. Structural risk escalating.

countries.name: Armenia
incidents: 25 Jun — Yerevan: US-Armenia strategic cooperation framework formally signed; Armenia's Western pivot now formalised; VP Vance visited Yerevan confirming US strategic partnership; CSTO withdrawal vote on track for Q3 2026.
23 Jun — Yerevan: Pashinyan's parliamentary election result confirmed; mandate for Western integration strategy intact; Russia-Armenia relations at historic low.
21 Jun — Yerevan: Armenia formally distancing from CSTO; CSTO withdrawal legislation advancing through parliament.
20 Jun — Syunik Province: Minor LoC incident on Armenia-Azerbaijan boundary; EUMA monitors confirm; no casualties; border demarcation process continuing.
countries.coa: Armenia pivoting firmly toward EU and US; Pashinyan electoral mandate confirmed. US-Armenia strategic framework formalised this week. Risk of renewed Azerbaijani military action moderate but declining as peace process advances. CSTO formal withdrawal likely within 12 months.

countries.name: Azerbaijan
incidents: 27 Jun — Baku: Aliyev monitoring US-Armenia strategic framework with caution; Zangezur corridor demands reiterated.
25 Jun — Baku: Azerbaijan monitoring Lebanon framework agreement; AZ-Iran border relations stable amid MOU implementation; energy and transport cooperation cautiously advancing.
23 Jun — AZ-Armenia Border: Bilateral border demarcation working group meets; Tavush sector demarcation advancing on schedule.
21 Jun — Baku: AZ-Iran diplomatic contacts resumed following Burgenstock round-two; energy cooperation agenda reactivated.
countries.coa: Azerbaijan leverages its strategic position confidently. Zangezur corridor remains primary diplomatic focus. AZ-Iran relations normalising further. US-Armenia framework creates a potential new constraint on Azerbaijani pressure. No major military action expected within 90 days.

regions.name: Sahel
overview: Sudan El Obeid siege at critical juncture: RSF drone strikes on El Obeid fuel station June 26 killed 1 civilian, wounded 4; RSF encirclement from north, south, and west tightening; 50+ civilians killed in 10 days; SAF drone strikes disrupting but not defeating RSF assault preparations; UN warns 'window is closing'; 38+ NGOs urge UN Human Rights Council bold atrocity prevention steps.

Nigeria Benue mass atrocity and Borno counter-operations: June 13-14 Yelwata attack killed 59+ civilians (residents report 100+); 53 suspects arrested June 26-27; Boko Haram kills 5 in Borno Magumeri; Nigerian troops kill 40 militants in Ngala; Nigeria-US June 24 intelligence strike kills 21 ISWAP fighters.

Burkina Faso Dori siege acute: Hospital surgical capacity exhausted; MSF withdrawn from 5 districts affecting 1.2 million people; information blackouts and journalist detentions signal peak junta fragility; Wagner support stretched thin across Mali and Burkina simultaneously.

Mali JNIM Bamako encirclement accelerating: Fuel rationing extended to 10 additional districts; food supply critical in 5 regions; JNIM drone strikes on FAMa supply convoys at 90+ since 2025; FAMa cannot dislodge JNIM without full Wagner reinforcement already spread thin.

countries.name: Mali
incidents: 26 Jun — Mopti Region: JNIM armed drone strikes FAMa supply convoy near Djenné; 3 soldiers killed, vehicles destroyed; drone strikes now at approximately 90+ in 2025-2026.
24 Jun — Bamako: Fuel rationing extended to 10 additional districts; food supply critical in 5 regions; JNIM supply-line stranglehold tightening toward Bamako.
22 Jun — Kidal: JNIM and FLA consolidate Kidal region; FAMa has no presence within 80km; junta cannot project force to northeast.
20 Jun — Gao: Joint JNIM-FLA coordinated attack on civilian-military convoy in Gao-Mopti corridor; 4 soldiers killed, 3 vehicles destroyed.
countries.coa: JNIM's Bamako encirclement strategy continues tightening — fuel and food rationing expanding to additional districts. Drone strike pace confirms JNIM's growing offensive capacity. FAMa cannot dislodge JNIM without full Wagner reinforcement already spread thin. Bamako encirclement risk within 90 days remains HIGH.

countries.name: Niger
incidents: 27 Jun — Niamey: JNIM June 18 airport attack aftermath — 22 attackers killed, 20 arrested; investigation ongoing into wider network; AES regional alliance headquarters at Diori Hamani Airport conducting damage assessment.
25 Jun — Dosso Region: JNIM patrol presence near Kirtachi maintained following June 14 National Guard camp overrun; CNSP reinforcements present but insufficient to dislodge.
23 Jun — Tillabéri: ISSP attack on civilian security convoy; 5 killed; CNSP emergency response deployed.
21 Jun — Diffa: Cross-border ISWAP incursion from Lake Chad zone; 2 villages raided, 500+ displaced.
countries.coa: The June 18 Niamey airport attack — second capital infrastructure strike of 2026 — confirms JNIM's strategic ambition and capability to strike the AES regional command hub. JNIM Dosso presence maintains the closest jihadist expansion to coastal West Africa. Niger faces a multi-axis threat with no effective Western counter-terrorism partnership. CRITICAL unchanged.

countries.name: Burkina Faso
incidents: 27 Jun — Dori (Sahel Region): JNIM siege of Dori continues; ICRC reports all surgical capability exhausted at Dori hospital; 420,000 people in affected area without external health services; no resupply route available.
25 Jun — Ouagadougou: Rolling 12-hour internet blackouts continue; 8 additional journalists detained this week; information suppression intensifying as regime fragility grows.
23 Jun — Est Region: MSF formally suspended all operations in 5 districts following repeated targeting of humanitarian vehicles; 1.2 million people affected by withdrawal.
21 Jun — Boucle du Mouhoun: Additional villages abandoned as JNIM expands into new areas; IDP camp overcrowding reaching critical threshold.
countries.coa: Burkina Faso's trajectory is the most severe in the region. Dori siege humanitarian phase is acute — surgical capacity exhausted, MSF fully withdrawn from 5 districts. Information blackouts signal peak regime fragility. No stabilisation pathway within 90 days; junta survival depends on Wagner support already stretched thin.

countries.name: Sudan
incidents: 27 Jun — El Obeid (North Kordofan): UN warns 'window is closing to prevent wider escalation'; 38+ NGOs urge UN Human Rights Council to take 'bold steps toward atrocity prevention'; HRW calls for urgent address of El Obeid situation.

26 Jun — El Obeid: RSF drone strike on central fuel station kills 1 civilian, wounds 4; several civilian vehicles destroyed; RSF encirclement from north, south, and west tightening; SAF drone strikes launched to foil RSF assault preparations.

24 Jun — El Obeid: RSF encirclement tightens around 500,000+ civilians; 50+ civilians killed by RSF drone strikes in prior 10 days; UN Security Council June 20 statement warns of 'imminent risk of mass atrocities'; Western nations demand RSF halt assault.

23 Jun — London/Washington: 30+ Western countries issue joint statement warning RSF against assault on El Obeid; seven European nations issue dedicated statement; SAF intensive drone strikes foiled one RSF ground assault preparation but encirclement unbroken.
countries.coa: El Obeid is in acute crisis — RSF drone strikes on fuel station and power infrastructure have disabled civilian services; RSF encirclement is now three-sided. SAF counter-drone operations disrupted one imminent ground assault but cannot break the encirclement. International warnings multiply without enforcement mechanism. If El Obeid falls, RSF gains the Kordofan-Darfur corridor — a potentially decisive strategic shift.

countries.name: Chad
incidents: 27 Jun — Lake Chad (Lac Province): ISWAP attack on Bol fishing community; 3 soldiers and 2 civilians killed; Lake Chad cross-border infiltration activity continuing.
25 Jun — Tibesti Region: ANT operation against armed group with Libya connections; 6 fighters killed.
23 Jun — Lake Chad: Joint Nigeria-Chad border security operations; ISWAP cross-lake infiltration routes under interdiction.
21 Jun — N'Djamena: Transitional council offers no new election timeline; democratic transition prospects receding.
countries.coa: Chad maintains relative stability as a regional anchor. ISWAP Lake Chad operations continue at manageable pace. Mahamat Déby continues delaying elections — no democratic transition within 90 days.

countries.name: Mauritania
incidents: 27 Jun — Hodh Ech Chargui: Security forces intercept JNIM-linked arms cache near Néma; 3 arrested; surveillance elevated following Niamey airport attack and ongoing Burkina/Mali crisis.
25 Jun — Néma: Additional military assets deployed to eastern border; intelligence sharing with Niger on JNIM airport attack network activated.
23 Jun — Nouakchott: Emergency security council meeting; counter-terrorism cooperation with EU expanded.
21 Jun — Nouakchott: Mauritanian government confirms eastern border risk elevated following JNIM capital-strike demonstration in Niamey.
countries.coa: Mauritania remains the most stable Sahel state. The JNIM Niamey airport attack adds urgency to eastern border security. JNIM drone proliferation and capital-strike capability add new threat vectors not yet reached Mauritanian territory. No kinetic escalation expected within 90 days.

countries.name: Nigeria
incidents: 27 Jun — Borno (Magumeri/Ngala LGA): Boko Haram kills 5 civilians in Magumeri; Nigerian Army counter-offensive kills approximately 40 Boko Haram militants in Ngala; Lake Chad corridor interdiction intensified.

26 Jun — Benue (Guma LGA): Authorities arrest 53 suspects including 26 linked to June 13-14 Yelwata village attack; state government confirms 59 killed, residents report over 100 dead — one of deadliest herder-farmer attacks of 2026.
24 Jun — Borno (Biu LGA): Nigeria-US intelligence-led strike kills 21 ISWAP fighters; disrupts terror logistics network; Lake Chad crossing corridors under intensified interdiction pressure.
21 Jun — Borno (Kukawa LGA): Nigerian Army rescues 47 ISWAP captives — mostly women and children — held in Kangarwa; 2 fighters killed in operation.
countries.coa: Nigeria's ISWAP threat remains CRITICAL. The June 13-14 Benue herder attack killing 59+ civilians confirms escalating communal violence alongside the jihadist threat. The June 24 Nigeria-US intelligence strike and June 21 Kukawa captive rescue are significant operational successes but ISWAP's mass-casualty capacity remains undiminished.

regions.name: Asia-Pacific
overview: North Korea June 26 weapons tests on Korean War anniversary: Kim Jong Un oversaw tests of special mission ballistic missile warhead, upgraded multiple rocket launcher (extended range), and self-propelled gun-howitzer on the 76th anniversary of the Korean War; 9th+ weapons test event of 2026; capabilities aimed at ROK border deployment; ROK and US assessing combined deterrence response.

Myanmar AA near total Rakhine control: AA seized Battalion 566 June 27, pushing junta forces to Thandwe airport and town hotels; AA controls 14 of 17 Rakhine townships; Kyaukphyu battle zone doubled in size as junta counteroffensive launched; China's $1.3B Kyaukphyu port investment under direct siege pressure; complete AA control of Rakhine is imminent.

China multi-theatre grey-zone expansion: Floating barrier at Scarborough Shoal confirmed operational within Philippine EEZ; expanded patrols east of Taiwan continue; CCG water cannon 6th+ confrontation at Scarborough in June; Vietnam-China incident frequency growing per ship-tracking data; 500+ US-Philippines exercises in 2026 as deterrent.

US-Philippines-Taiwan deepening security ties: Philippines deepened unofficial security ties with Taiwan — combined Bashi Channel coast guard patrols and information sharing; Japan-Philippines maritime boundary talks prompted China's retaliatory patrol announcement east of Taiwan; US-Philippines-Australia joint South China Sea patrol conducted.

countries.name: North Korea
incidents: 26 Jun — DPRK: Kim Jong Un oversees weapons tests on 76th anniversary of Korean War start; tests include special mission ballistic missile warhead, upgraded multiple rocket launcher with extended range, and self-propelled gun-howitzer; state media calls demonstrations part of 'irreversible' military modernisation programme.
24 Jun — East Sea: DPRK submarine activity observed east of Korean Peninsula; JMSDF and ROK Navy tracking; China-DPRK military cooperation from Xi June 8-9 state visit being operationalised.

22 Jun — Seoul: ROK NSC updates combined deterrence response following June 15 tactical cruise missile test; THAAD enhancement deployment timelines accelerated; US-ROK-Japan trilateral meeting scheduled.
20 Jun — DPRK: ROK intelligence assesses technical failure in reported MIRV ICBM test June 18; DPRK maintains active missile development pace across multiple platforms.
countries.coa: North Korea's June 26 tests — special mission ballistic missile warhead, upgraded rocket launcher, self-propelled gun-howitzer — confirm continued multi-platform development timed to strategic anniversaries. Tactical cruise missiles designated for ROK border deployment remain the near-term military indicator. Denuclearisation probability in 90 days: zero. Additional tests expected.

countries.name: Myanmar
incidents: 27 Jun — Rakhine State (Thandwe): AA seizes headquarters of junta Battalion 566; junta forces retreat to Thandwe airport and hotels inside the city; AA claims 400+ junta troops killed in Thandwe offensive with large arms haul captured.

26 Jun — Rakhine State (Kyaukphyu): Battle zones around Kyaukphyu doubled in size as junta reinforced and launched counteroffensive; AA surrounding western naval bases; China's $1.3B port investment under direct siege pressure.

24 Jun — Rakhine State: AA controls 14 of 17 townships; last three — Sittwe, Kyaukphyu, Manaung — targeted for capture; AA commander vows to keep fighting until 'final victory'; India and Bangladesh maintaining direct communications with AA as de facto Rakhine authority.
22 Jun — Shan State: SCEF resistance alliance captures additional territory near Lashio; junta airstrike kills 8 civilians; multi-front collapse of junta control accelerating.
countries.coa: AA's June 27 capture of Battalion 566 HQ confirms Thandwe is nearly under full AA control. AA controls 14 of 17 townships. India and Bangladesh have both opened direct communications with AA as de facto Rakhine authority. China faces maximum diplomatic pressure to engage AA given Kyaukphyu port investment at risk. Full junta expulsion from Rakhine is the most likely near-term outcome.

countries.name: Pakistan
incidents: 25 Jun — KPK (North Waziristan): TTP attack on military checkpost; 4 security forces killed; cross-border coordination from Afghanistan confirmed.
23 Jun — Balochistan: BLA attack on gas pipeline infrastructure; engineers evacuated, pipeline damaged; energy infrastructure targeting accelerating.
21 Jun — Peshawar: Security services arrest 4 TTP facilitators in coordinated raids; cross-border coordination network partially disrupted.
20 Jun — LoC (Kashmir): Cross-border firing incident; India-Pakistan ceasefire technically intact at 13+ months.
countries.coa: Pakistan faces simultaneous TTP pressure in KPK and BLA infrastructure attacks in Balochistan. India-Pakistan ceasefire holds at 13+ months but remains structurally fragile. Economic deterioration compounds security challenges.

countries.name: India
incidents: 27 Jun — New Delhi: India-US defence cooperation meeting; focus on LAC threat posture and Pakistan-based militant networks; US-India intelligence sharing enhanced.
25 Jun — Kashmir (Kupwara): Security forces kill 3 militants in gunfight; 2 CRPF troopers wounded; Pakistan-based militant infiltration continuing.
23 Jun — Arunachal Pradesh: PLA patrol activity near Asaphila sector intensifies; India maintains heightened LAC monitoring posture.
21 Jun — Manipur: Ethnic violence continues; 3 killed in Churachandpur district; security forces maintaining curfew in 5 districts.
countries.coa: India maintains elevated military posture vis-à-vis both Pakistan and China. Manipur ethnic conflict drains domestic security resources. LAC tensions are a persistent secondary concern. India-Pakistan ceasefire remains structurally fragile.

countries.name: China
incidents: 27 Jun — South China Sea: CCG floating barrier at Scarborough Shoal confirmed operational within Philippine EEZ; Philippines files 7th diplomatic protest of June; China-Vietnam incident frequency growing per Bloomberg ship-tracking data.

25 Jun — Taiwan Strait/Pacific: Beijing maintains expanded law enforcement patrols east of Taiwan announced June 25 — retaliatory measure against Japan-Philippines maritime boundary delimitation talks; Japan expresses formal concern.
23 Jun — South China Sea: CCG water cannon deployed against Philippines coast guard at Scarborough Shoal — 6th confrontation of June; Liaoning Carrier Strike Group concluded 40-day deployment.
21 Jun — Yellow Sea: CCG incursion into South Korean EEZ; Seoul summoned Chinese ambassador for fourth time this month; PLA Southern Theatre Command conducted separate naval drills near Luzon.
countries.coa: China's grey-zone expansion is simultaneously multi-theatre — Yellow Sea, Scarborough Shoal, Taiwan Strait expanded patrols, Liaoning strike group demonstrating regional reach, and Vietnam incidents growing. Beijing's retaliatory posture toward Japan-Philippines boundary talks signals intensifying territorial assertiveness. No major military action vs. Taiwan expected within 90 days but grey-zone intensity increasing.

countries.name: Taiwan
incidents: 27 Jun — Taiwan Strait: China's expanded patrols east of Taiwan continue; Philippines deepened unofficial security ties with Taipei including combined Bashi Channel coast guard patrols and information sharing.
25 Jun — Taiwan ADIZ: PLA incursion by 11 aircraft into Taiwan ADIZ; fighters and naval assets scrambled; monthly PLA detection rates remain at high levels.
23 Jun — Pratas/Dongsha: CCG and PRC research vessel coordinated patrol concluded; 8th Dongsha incursion of 2026.
21 Jun — Kinmen Islands: CCG intrusion into Kinmen restricted waters; Taiwan coast guard issues formal warning; defence budget amendment passed after opposition reversal.
countries.coa: Taiwan faces sustained and intensifying grey-zone pressure including China's new announced patrols east of Taiwan. Philippines deepening unofficial security ties with Taipei adds a new deterrent dimension. Taiwan's defence budget amendment passage removes a structural vulnerability. Cross-strait kinetic conflict risk is LOW in 90-day window but grey-zone intensity increasing.

countries.name: Philippines
incidents: 27 Jun — South China Sea: Satellite imagery confirms China's floating barrier at Scarborough Shoal entrance inside Philippine EEZ fully operational; Philippines files 7th diplomatic protest of June.

25 Jun — South China Sea: Philippines, US, and Australia conduct joint South China Sea patrol; Philippines deepening unofficial security ties with Taiwan including combined Bashi Channel coast guard patrols.
23 Jun — Scarborough Shoal: CCG water cannon deployed against Philippines coast guard — 6th Scarborough confrontation of June.
21 Jun — Second Thomas Shoal: Philippines conducts resupply mission to BRP Sierra Madre; CCG shadow operation maintains confrontational posture but resupply succeeds.
countries.coa: Philippines faces persistent and escalating Chinese grey-zone pressure. Floating barrier at Scarborough Shoal is a significant new infrastructure escalation. US-Philippines-Australia joint patrol and 500+ planned US-Philippines exercises strengthen deterrent posture. Scarborough Shoal miscalculation risk remains elevated.

countries.name: South Korea
incidents: 27 Jun — Seoul: ROK Joint Chiefs of Staff assessing June 26 DPRK weapons tests; additional US-ROK-Japan trilateral deterrence coordination underway; THAAD enhancement on accelerated timeline.
25 Jun — Seoul: ROK ministry reports 4th CCG incursion into South Korean Yellow Sea EEZ this month; Chinese ambassador summoned for fourth time.
23 Jun — Seoul: ROK NSC updates combined deterrence response following DPRK June 15 tactical cruise missile test.
21 Jun — DMZ: DPRK military activity near DMZ at elevated monitoring status following June 15 test; June 26 weapons tests add new assessment requirements.
countries.coa: South Korea faces an expanding threat matrix — DPRK's June 26 special mission ballistic missile warhead test and China Yellow Sea grey-zone expansion simultaneously. ROK will accelerate US-ROK combined deterrence, THAAD enhancement, and Japan trilateral coordination.

countries.name: Vietnam
incidents: 27 Jun — South China Sea: Bloomberg ship-tracking data confirms China-Vietnam incident frequency continuing to grow; Vietnam lodged 4th+ diplomatic protest over CCG water cannon use on Vietnamese fishing vessels.
25 Jun — South China Sea: Vietnam coast guard confronts Chinese fishing fleet inside EEZ; vessels withdraw after formal warning.
23 Jun — Hanoi: Vietnam-US maritime domain awareness operations coordination expanded following Balikatan 2026.
20 Jun — South China Sea: Vietnamese coast guard confronts Chinese fishing fleet inside EEZ; 'Bamboo diplomacy' maintained; US-Vietnam defence cooperation deepening.
countries.coa: Vietnam faces persistent and escalating grey-zone friction with China — ship-tracking data confirms growing incident frequency. 'Bamboo diplomacy' maintained; US-Vietnam defence cooperation deepening. No kinetic escalation expected within 90 days.

countries.name: Japan
incidents: 27 Jun — Tokyo: Japan assessing June 26 DPRK weapons tests; Japan-US-ROK trilateral deterrence coordination response underway; counter-strike capability programme on accelerated timeline.
25 Jun — Tokyo: Beijing maintains expanded sea patrols east of Taiwan in response to Japan-Philippines maritime boundary talks; Japan-Philippines bilateral security cooperation deepening.
23 Jun — Tokyo: Japan participated in Balikatan 2026 at unprecedented level; Japan-US-ROK trilateral deterrence coordination accelerating.
21 Jun — East China Sea: MSDF destroyer deployed to monitor PLA naval transit following Liaoning deployment conclusion.
countries.coa: Japan faces a deteriorating strategic environment — DPRK June 26 weapons tests and China's retaliatory patrol announcement — but no imminent kinetic threat. Counter-strike capability programme accelerating. Japan-US alliance remains the primary deterrent.

countries.name: Indonesia
incidents: 25 Jun — Natuna Sea: Indonesian coast guard confronts Chinese fishing fleet inside EEZ; 7 vessels warned off.
22 Jun — Papua: OPM separatists fire on security patrol; 1 soldier lightly wounded.
countries.coa: Indonesia maintaining non-aligned posture while quietly strengthening maritime security. Natuna Sea incursions are a persistent Chinese grey-zone tactic. Papua OPM remains a low-level internal concern.

countries.name: Thailand
incidents: 25 Jun — Deep South: Suspected separatist IED attack on security patrol; 1 soldier injured.
23 Jun — Bangkok: MARA Patani dialogue sixth round preparation underway; limited framework progress.
countries.coa: Thailand's Deep South separatist conflict remains a contained low-level challenge. MARA Patani dialogue showing limited progress. Bangkok's political stability has improved.

countries.name: Bangladesh
incidents: 27 Jun — Dhaka: Interim government security operations against JMB networks continue; 5 suspects arrested.
25 Jun — Cox's Bazar: Rohingya camp inter-gang violence injures 6; UN access restricted for 8 hours.
countries.coa: Bangladesh faces internal governance challenges post-2024 transition. Rohingya crisis remains a persistent instability source. External threat level is LOW.

countries.name: Sri Lanka
incidents: 25 Jun — Colombo: IMF sixth review assessment advancing; government on track with fiscal targets.
23 Jun — Northern Province: Tamil political dialogue with government continues; land restitution process advancing.
countries.coa: Sri Lanka is in economic recovery mode post-2022 crisis. Security environment stable. Chinese debt diplomacy remains a structural strategic competition concern.

regions.name: Americas
overview: Colombia 'total peace' ended — ELN operations resuming: De la Espriella won runoff June 21 with 49.65%; ELN ceasefire expired June 23; attacks reported in Arauca and Norte de Santander within hours; new president assumes office August 7 promising no peace negotiations with armed groups; US-Colombia counter-insurgency cooperation to deepen significantly.

Cuba economic collapse deepening: Cuban National Assembly enacted new economic laws June 19; food production at 40% of normal; medicine at 30%; infant mortality risen to 9.9/1,000 births (pre-crisis 4.0); eastern Cuba protests spreading geographically; US Visa/Mastercard block eliminates last significant tourism revenue; government suppression capacity under strain.

Haiti GSF crisis unresolved: GSF at 25% of mandated strength with Chadian officers at Camp Vertières; Viv Ansanm expanding gang territorial control in Artibonite agricultural zone; 2,300+ dead since start of 2026; 1.5 million displaced; US travel ban on Haitian nationals in effect from June 9.

Venezuela post-intervention stabilization advancing: Tren de Aragua leader Niño Guerrero killed June 12-13 in joint US-Venezuela operation; 47-state recognition achieved; oil infrastructure at ~60% pre-intervention capacity; civilian frustration with US-influenced transition growing; Maduro loyalist sabotage cells active.

countries.name: Venezuela
incidents: 27 Jun — Caracas: Maduro loyalist sabotage cell activity at Zulia oil refineries continuing; oil infrastructure at approximately 60% capacity; US investors showing declining confidence in regulatory environment.

25 Jun — Maracaibo: Zulia refinery restoration operations continuing under US-Venezuela joint security coordination; sabotage incidents declining but persistent; civilian frustration with US-influenced transition growing.

23 Jun — Caracas: 47-state recognition of transitional authority achieved; international coordination improving; White House influence over transitional decision-making drawing international criticism.
21 Jun — Apure: Colombia-Venezuela joint border security operation; 4 armed group members killed; border clearing operations continuing.
countries.coa: Venezuela's post-intervention stabilisation is advancing — Tren de Aragua leadership killed, 47-state recognition achieved, oil infrastructure restoring. However, civilian frustration with the US-influenced transition is growing as regulatory environment remains unpredictable. Maduro loyalist sabotage cells remain active at oil infrastructure. CRITICAL maintained; downgrade to HIGH possible within 90 days if oil restoration and democratic roadmap progress.

countries.name: Haiti
incidents: 27 Jun — Artibonite: Gang expansion in Artibonite agricultural zone continues; 3,500+ farmers displaced; food supply disruption worsening across northern Haiti; USAID emergency allocation announced for Artibonite food crisis.
25 Jun — Port-au-Prince: GSF conducting targeted operations in Tabarre and Pétion-Ville; 3 gang members killed; GSF remains at 25% of mandated 5,500 strength.
23 Jun — Port-au-Prince: 2,300+ killed in gang violence since start of 2026; 1.5 million Haitians displaced; 90% of Port-au-Prince under gang control; US travel ban on Haitian nationals in effect.
21 Jun — Cité Soleil: HNP-GSF joint operation frees 6 kidnap victims; 2 gang members killed; capacity constraints limiting sustained offensive operations.
countries.coa: GSF remains critically understaffed at 25% of mandated strength. Viv Ansanm's Artibonite expansion threatens food security and is driving additional displacement. Additional urban centres at risk within 90 days unless GSF deployment dramatically accelerates.

countries.name: Colombia
incidents: 27 Jun — Arauca/Norte de Santander: ELN operations resuming across traditional strongholds following June 23 ceasefire expiry; military assessing scope of initial attacks; EMC continues independent operations.
26 Jun — Cauca: EMC attack kills 2 security forces; military airstrike on EMC position; EMC operations independent of ELN ceasefire dynamics.
24 Jun — Bogotá: De la Espriella confirmed winner with 12.9M votes (49.65%); gives illegal armed groups one month to surrender to justice; signals no peace negotiations; assumes office August 7.
23 Jun — Arauca/Norte de Santander: ELN ceasefire expires at midnight; first ELN attacks reported within hours; Colombia's 'total peace' experiment effectively over.
countries.coa: De la Espriella's confirmed win and ELN ceasefire expiry on June 23 marks the end of Colombia's 'total peace' experiment. ELN full operations resuming across Arauca and Norte de Santander. EMC and EMBF continue regardless. US-Colombia counter-insurgency cooperation will deepen significantly from August 7. Colombia's conflict will intensify before military pressure produces results.

countries.name: Cuba
incidents: 27 Jun — Eastern Cuba: Protest movement continues at furthest eastward extent; government deploys additional rapid-response units; suppression capacity showing strain; arrests continuing.
25 Jun — Santiago de Cuba: Power outages trigger demonstrations across eastern Cuba for sixth consecutive week; rapid-response units deployed; 15 arrested in latest round.

23 Jun — Havana: UN OHCHR reports infant mortality risen to 9.9/1,000 births (pre-crisis 4.0); food production at 40% of normal; medicine at 30%; US Visa/Mastercard block eliminates last significant tourism revenue.

21 Jun — Havana: National Assembly June 19 enacted new economic laws aimed at restructuring amid blockade; economic crisis acknowledged publicly; OHCHR calls conditions an acute humanitarian emergency.
countries.coa: Cuba's economic and humanitarian crisis is at its deepest point since the 1990s 'Special Period.' US blocking of Visa/Mastercard and Venezuelan oil cutoff have eliminated major revenue channels. Protest movement expanding geographically. Government suppressing militarily but capacity constraints growing. Risk of regime-threatening upheaval within 6-12 months is elevated and rising.

countries.name: Ecuador
incidents: 27 Jun — Esmeraldas: Multi-cartel conflict kills 4 including 1 police officer; state of exception extended for sixth consecutive period.
25 Jun — Guayaquil: Los Lobos-R7 cartel reprisal cycle continues; 3 killed.
23 Jun — Esmeraldas: Cross-border criminal network with Colombia-linked groups disrupted; 2 arrested.
21 Jun — Quito: Government security council reports June homicide rate tracking above May averages.
countries.coa: Ecuador faces entrenched cartel violence that has outpaced state capacity. State of exception provides temporary relief but no structural improvement expected within 90 days.

countries.name: Peru
incidents: 27 Jun — Lima: Congress debates emergency security measures; Boluarte government fragile but functional.
25 Jun — VRAEM: SL-VRAEM attack on rural security patrol; 2 officers killed.
23 Jun — Lima: Anti-government protests over economic conditions; 4,000 demonstrators; peaceful.
21 Jun — Loreto: 2 killed in illegal mining territorial dispute near Colombian border.
countries.coa: Peru's challenges remain primarily internal: SL-VRAEM insurgency, illegal mining violence, and political instability. Boluarte government fragile but functional. No external threat.

countries.name: Bolivia
incidents: 27 Jun — La Paz: Arce-Morales factional conflict deepens; congressional investigation expanding scope.
25 Jun — Santa Cruz: Civic committee protests continue over economic grievances; government mobilises security forces.
23 Jun — Chapare: Cocalero protests ease; alternative development funding dispute temporarily resolved.
21 Jun — Cochabamba: MAS internal convention postponed amid security concerns; factional tensions escalating.
countries.coa: Bolivia faces political instability rather than security crisis. MAS internal factional conflict creates governance uncertainty. No armed conflict at scale expected within 90 days.

countries.name: Dominican Republic
incidents: 27 Jun — Dajabón: Border trade continues restricted due to Haitian gang advance in Artibonite zone; Dominican Republic security forces maintain heightened vigilance.
25 Jun — Santo Domingo: Government deports 2,400 irregular migrants in biweekly operation; Haiti border closures extended amid Artibonite gang expansion.
countries.coa: Dominican Republic faces Haiti spillover risks but maintains internal security stability. Primary monitoring requirement: Haitian gang advance toward Dominican border zone.

countries.name: Jamaica
incidents: 27 Jun — Kingston: West Kingston state of emergency continues; crime statistics show 24% reduction vs. prior year period.
25 Jun — Montego Bay: Tourism zone security operation arrests 3 gang members; tourist facilities unaffected.
countries.coa: Jamaica faces persistent gang violence in Kingston. State of emergency showing positive crime reduction trend. Organised crime is the primary concern; no geopolitical threat.
